content:
🔥 3dsvg | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling developers to easily convert static SVG files into interactive 3D components within React applications, and providing a user-friendly visual editor for creating and exporting 3D assets.
✨ Key Features: • Embeddable `` React component • Visual editor for designing 3D objects from SVGs • Multiple input methods (Text, Pixel Editor, SVG Code, File Upload) • 10 material presets and procedural/custom textures • 7 animation options
📖 Summary: 3dsvg is a monorepo providing a React component for creating interactive 3D visualizations from SVGs and a web-based visual editor to design and export these 3D objects. It simplifies the process of turning 2D vector graphics into dynamic 3D experiences.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 donutbrowser | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Managing multiple, isolated browser environments for tasks requiring distinct digital footprints, such as web scraping, testing, or maintaining anonymity online.
✨ Key Features: • Unlimited isolated browser profiles • Chromium & Firefox engines with advanced fingerprint spoofing • Proxy and VPN support per profile • Local API & MCP for automation
📖 Summary: Donut Browser is an open-source anti-detect browser built with Rust, offering robust privacy and profile management. It allows users to create and manage unlimited isolated browser profiles, each with its own unique fingerprint, cookies, and extensions, powered by Chromium and Firefox engines with advanced spoofing capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 KrillinAI | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automated video translation and dubbing for creating multilingual, cross-platform video content.
✨ Key Features: • One-click deployment and operation • Video acquisition via yt-dlp or local upload • High-accuracy speech recognition (Whisper) • LLM-powered subtitle segmentation and translation • Professional vocabulary replacement
📖 Summary: KrillinAI is a Go-based tool for video translation and dubbing, offering end-to-end localization for cross-platform content. It supports over 100 languages, integrates speech recognition and LLM-based translation, and features voice cloning for professional-sounding dubs.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 qxresearch-event-1 | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To serve as a learning resource for Python developers, showcasing how to build various applications with minimal code, and to provide easily understandable examples for different programming concepts.
✨ Key Features: • 50+ Python applications • 10 lines of code per application • Covers ML, DL, GUI, CV, API development
📖 Summary: This repository offers a collection of over 50 practical Python applications, each written with a maximum of 10 lines of code. It covers diverse areas like Machine Learning, GUI, Computer Vision, and API development, aiming to provide simple, understandable, and easily modifiable code examples for learning and experimentation.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 TREK | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide individuals and groups with a comprehensive, self-hosted platform for planning and managing all aspects of their trips, from initial itinerary creation to budgeting and packing.
✨ Key Features: • Self-hosted • Real-time collaboration • Interactive maps (Leaflet/Mapbox GL) • Budget tracker • Packing lists
📖 Summary: TREK is a self-hosted, real-time collaborative travel planner application. It offers features like interactive maps, budget tracking, packing lists, and a journal, all accessible via a Progressive Web App (PWA) with support for Single Sign-On (SSO).
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
